announcement.text: Building practical systems across AI, web, real-time data, and immersive 3D interfaces.

hero.name: Yassir Aitali
hero.title: Software Engineer
hero.description: I build modern software systems where AI workflows, web platforms, real-time data, and interactive 3D meet — from full-stack apps and dashboards to LLM/RAG tools and Unity/WebGL digital-twin interfaces.
hero.availability: Open to selected software engineering roles, technical collaborations, and product-driven projects.

about.title: About Me
about.description: I'm a software engineer finishing my final-year degree at EMSI, currently completing a 6-month PFE internship at UM6P's Digital Innovation Center (DICE) on an industrial digital-twin platform. I work across the stack and across disciplines: AI-enabled applications (LLM integration, RAG, agents), full-stack web (React/TypeScript, Vue, Node), real-time 3D and digital-twin interfaces (Unity/WebGL), and computer vision.
about.objective: I use AI both as a subject I understand and as an engineering tool — from prompting and retrieval pipelines to AI-assisted development — while keeping control over architecture, quality, and security. My recent work spans an industrial digital twin, a real-estate 3D platform, a computer-vision smart-parking system, and WebGL body tracking. I care about systems that move from prototype to something real people can actually use.

snapshot.title: Snapshot
snapshot.pfe: PFE @ UM6P DICE — Digital Innovation Center
snapshot.languages: TypeScript · JavaScript · Python · C# · Java · C++ · PHP · SQL

whatIBuild.title: What I Build
whatIBuild.subtitle: Four areas where I deliver end-to-end.

items.title: AI Engineering & Automation
items.description: LLM integration, RAG systems, AI agents, prompt engineering, document automation, and AI-assisted development workflows.

items.title: Full-Stack Web Platforms
items.description: React/Next.js & Vue front-ends, Node APIs, databases, dashboards, admin tools, authentication, deployment-ready apps.

items.title: Real-Time 3D & Digital Twins
items.description: Unity/C#, WebGL, Three.js, live data-driven 3D supervision, virtual tours, digital-twin interfaces.

items.title: Computer Vision
items.description: Body/pose tracking, detection pipelines, image analysis feeding real-time interfaces.

positions.role: Software Engineer Intern (PFE) — Digital Twin, Real-Time Systems & 3D Interfaces
positions.company: UM6P DICE — Digital Innovation Center of Excellence
positions.description: Final-year PFE building an industrial digital-twin platform: linking physical zones, virtual entities, and live state into a real-time 3D supervision interface.
achievements: Designed digital-twin logic linking physical zones, virtual entities, and live state
Built Unity/3D supervision interfaces for industrial monitoring
Worked with real-time communication concepts (MQTT/WebSocket-style) and monitoring views
Produced technical documentation and project structure

positions.description: Shipped immersive web-based 3D property tours and the build pipeline to deliver them at scale.
achievements: Delivered immersive 3D property tours in Unity (C#) deployed to WebGL, with build optimization (cloud storage, compression, caching)
Modeled and integrated 3D environments in Blender / Maya, with GPU optimization and a modular project architecture
Built 3D web experiences with Three.js and TypeScript, managing assets through a GitHub CI/CD pipeline
Implemented dynamic loading (lazy loading, scalable structure) for smooth large-scene delivery

positions.description: Built a real-time body-tracking pipeline from camera to interactive 3D, running in the browser.
achievements: Built a real-time body-tracking app on MediaPipe with a Python → Node.js (WebSocket) → Unity WebGL pipeline
Processed continuous tracking streams with normalization, filtering, and optimization
Designed a modular, event-driven architecture improving stability and maintainability
Optimized Unity (C#) WebGL rendering and deployed to UM6P's cloud for internal testing

positions.description: Built and optimized full-stack web solutions and structured REST APIs for production use.
achievements: Built full-stack web solutions (PHP, JS, MySQL) with REST APIs structured on an MVC architecture
Optimized backend performance and data handling with complex SQL queries and lightweight processing pipelines
Set up DevOps workflows: Git branching, Linux server deployment, and basic update automation
Improved reliability through application monitoring, advanced logging, and load optimization

projects.title: Selected Work
projects.subtitle: A few case studies that show how I work across AI, full-stack, and real-time 3D.

featured.title: Industrial Digital Twin Platform
featured.context: Final-year PFE at UM6P's Digital Innovation Center of Excellence (DICE), building an industrial digital-twin platform for Industry 4.0 supervision.
featured.problem: Operators need to monitor physical industrial zones, sensors, and alerts from a single intuitive interface that mirrors the real installation in 3D.
featured.built: A Unity 3D/VR supervision interface backed by a Python data layer, with semantic mapping of physical zones to virtual objects and real-time sensor/alert supervision.
featured.demonstrates: End-to-end digital-twin engineering: connecting physical state to a live 3D model and designing supervision UX for industrial data.
featured.status: PFE — high-level case study, no confidential data.

featured.title: AI Assistant / RAG System
featured.context: A document-intelligence application built to explore real AI engineering beyond simple API calls.
featured.problem: Teams sit on large document sets but can't quickly get trustworthy, source-grounded answers.
featured.built: An app to upload documents, ask questions, and get source-grounded answers and summaries, backed by a retrieval pipeline over embeddings.
featured.demonstrates: Real AI engineering — retrieval pipelines, grounding, and product thinking, not just calling an API.

featured.title: KEY ONE — Real-Estate 3D Visualization Platform
featured.context: A React/TypeScript real-estate platform for navigating properties in interactive 3D.
featured.problem: Buyers struggle to understand spatial layout and unit availability from static listings.
featured.built: Interactive 3D / hex-map property navigation and a visual unit planner for browsing and comparing units.
featured.demonstrates: Production-oriented front-end engineering with real-time 3D and complex interactive state.

featured.title: OPTIPARK — Computer-Vision Smart Parking
featured.context: A smart-parking system that detects free spaces from camera feeds and surfaces them on a live dashboard.
featured.problem: Drivers waste time hunting for free spaces; operators lack real-time occupancy data.
featured.built: A three-tier system: computer-vision detection feeds an API, which streams occupancy to a real-time monitoring dashboard.
featured.demonstrates: Full-stack systems thinking across CV, backend, and a live front-end dashboard.

featured.title: WebGL Body Tracking System
featured.tagline: Real-Time Pose Tracking in the Browser
featured.context: Real-time pose/body tracking running inside a Unity WebGL build, from an internship at UM6P's Vanguard Center.
featured.problem: Body-tracking experiences usually require a native app; this one targets the browser directly.
featured.built: A real-time pose/body tracking pipeline rendered in a Unity WebGL build, fed by live streamed data.
featured.demonstrates: Real-time computer vision integrated into interactive 3D and shipped to the browser.

featured.title: Blockchain Certificate Verification System
featured.context: A prototype for tamper-proof verification of credentials and certificates.
featured.problem: Paper and PDF certificates are easy to forge and hard to verify at scale.
featured.built: A credential-verification prototype using blockchain-backed integrity checks.
featured.demonstrates: Applying cryptographic integrity and verification to a real trust problem.

featured.title: REST Stack Performance Benchmark
featured.context: A benchmark harness comparing how REST stack choices behave under load.
featured.problem: Stack decisions are often made on intuition rather than measured trade-offs.
featured.built: A benchmark that measures how REST stack choices affect latency, throughput, and resource use.
featured.demonstrates: Engineering rigor — measuring trade-offs instead of guessing.
